spec.reasoning: Three safe anchor types are used: (1) Lexical — renamed 'Date' to 'Transaction Date', 'Fabric' to 'Material', and 'Units Sold' to 'Qty Sold', plus renamed the sheet from 'Sheet1' to 'SalesData'. None of these column names appear as general English words in the instruction body (the instruction uses 'date' generically but the column rename keeps 'Date' in the new name as 'Transaction Date', and the key columns H and I referenced in the instruction are unnamed lookup/answer columns unaffected by this rename). (2) Positional title row insert — inserts a title row at row 1, pushing headers from row 2 to row 3 and all data down by one row. This tests whether the model hardcodes row references. The instruction references column H and answer position I4:I7, which are column-based references that the framework will update accordingly. (3) Distractor column — added 'Unit Price' after the last column, a plausibly related but clearly different column from 'Qty Sold', to test if the model gets confused by extra columns. No data values needed renaming since the column renames don't overlap with data cell contents.
